title: NOC 32123 – Cardiology Technologists and Electrophysiological Diagnostic Technologists | GTR Immigration
description: Cardiology Technologists and Electrophysiological Diagnostic Technologists under NOC 32123 are specialized diagnostic professionals supporting cardiovascular and neurological healthcare services across Canada. With rising rates of cardiac disease, neurological disorders, and expanded diagnostic services post-pandemic, demand for this TEER 2 occupation remains steady nationwide. For qualified international professionals, NOC 32123 offers eligibility under Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs, providing a structured pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3212 – Medical technologists and techniciansNOC Code32123

duties: Operate ECG and cardiac monitoring equipment
Conduct stress tests and ambulatory monitoring
Analyze cardiac recordings and prepare preliminary reports
Assist in pacemaker testing and reprogramming
Conduct EEG, EMG, and nerve conduction studies
Monitor and record electrical activity of brain and nervous system
employment_requirements: Completion of a two-year college diploma program in cardiology technology
Registration with the Canadian Society of Cardiology Technologists (required in all provinces except Quebec)
Completion of a two-year college or hospital-based program in electrophysiology technology
Registration with the Canadian Board of Electroencephalograph Technologists (required in Alberta and usually required elsewhere)
Registration with the Association of Electromyography Technologists of Canada may be required
In Quebec, registration with Ordre des technologues en imagerie médicale, en radio-oncologie et en électrophysiologie médicale du Québec is mandatory.
Internationally educated applicants must complete credential recognition and certification prior to employment.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $42,400 to $73,200, depending on province and specialization.
job_outlook: Expansion of cardiac catheterization and electrophysiology services
Specialized technologists with advanced certification may experience stronger employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 32123 are employed in:
This balanced outlook reflects stable long-term employment prospects, particularly in provinces such as Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Quebec, and Nova Scotia.

pathways: If you are a qualified Cardiology Technologist or Electrophysiological Diagnostic Technologist, NOC 32123 may provide a pathway toward permanent residence in Canada.
